prompt:
Goal: Create a vertical editorial lifestyle portrait of a stylish young creative woman seated in a bright design studio corner, surrounded by inspirational creative notes, design tools, and soft collage-style paper elements.

Canvas: Portrait orientation, 2:3 aspect ratio, realistic fashion photography mixed with scrapbook mood-board styling. Soft daylight, warm neutral tones, gentle shadows on a gray concrete floor, off-white textured wall background.

Main subject: A full-body young woman sits centered on a modern white molded chair with wooden legs. She has long voluminous wavy dark brown hair, a softly obscured/non-detailed face, delicate earrings, a thin necklace, and a wristwatch. She wears a loose dark navy/black button-down shirt with sleeves casually rolled, high-waisted wide-leg white trousers, and chunky white slide sandals. Her legs are crossed, hands relaxed on her lap, posture calm and confident, looking forward.

Left side objects: Place exactly 1 vintage silver-and-black film camera on a black tripod to the woman’s left, lens facing forward. Behind it is exactly 1 torn dark navy poster reading “Be Different.” in large white serif type. Below the camera is exactly 1 spiral notebook page titled “Creative Cloud” listing exactly 6 app rows with small colored square icons and labels: Ps Photoshop, Ai Illustrator, Id InDesign, Ae After Effects, Pr Premiere Pro, Lr Lightroom. Below that is exactly 1 polaroid-style photo of a desk with coffee and creative supplies, captioned “Creativity is Fun.” with a hand-drawn heart. At bottom left, add exactly 1 torn dark navy paper quote reading “Design is thinking made visual.” with a rough brush stroke underneath.

Top and right wall collage: Add exactly 4 separate wall papers around the subject: 1 small taped photo above her head showing a desk lamp and minimal vase; 1 cream paper clipped with a black paperclip reading “Creativity takes courage.” with smaller attribution “– Henri Matisse”; 1 torn dark navy paper reading “Dare to Stand Out.” in white handwritten lettering with underline strokes and a small star; and the left “Be Different.” poster already counted above. Use visible tape pieces and slight paper shadows for a handmade mood-board look.

Right side objects: Place exactly 1 lush green potted plant in a light gray cylindrical pot to the woman’s right. Lean exactly 1 color palette card against the pot, showing exactly 5 horizontal swatches from very dark navy through mid grays to beige, with the handwritten label “Color Palette” at the bottom. On the floor at lower right, place exactly 1 spiral notebook page reading “IDEAS > PLANS > ACTION = REALITY” with a small hand-drawn star. Next to it place exactly 1 black pen cup holding several pens, and exactly 2 stacked design books labeled “KINFOLK” and “BAUHAUS.”

Visual style: Realistic high-resolution portrait with a soft magazine look, muted navy, cream, white, gray, beige, and green palette. Combine polished fashion photography with tactile paper textures, torn edges, tape, handwritten typography, serif quote typography, and cozy creative-studio styling.

Text constraints: Keep all visible text exactly as specified, legible, and in English. Do not add extra slogans, logos, watermarks, or additional people. Maintain the centered seated pose, crossed legs, white chair, vintage camera, plant, and exactly the listed count of collage papers and design props.
